heading: Restriction on importation or entry
label: 8303 Restriction on importation or entry
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of the Secretary of Agriculture relating to agricultural import and entry inspection activities under this chapter to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 231, 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6
amendments: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (c)(1)(B), was in the original “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle E (§§ 10401–10418) of title X of , , , which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of subtitle E to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 107–171116 Stat. 494section 8301 of this titleMay 13, 2002

children.text: the importation or entry of any animal, article, or means of conveyance, or use of any means of conveyance or facility, if the Secretary determines that the prohibition or restriction is necessary to prevent the introduction into or dissemination within the United States of any pest or disease of livestock;

the further movement of any animal that has strayed into the United States if the Secretary determines that the prohibition or restriction is necessary to prevent the introduction into or dissemination within the United States of any pest or disease of livestock; and

the use of any means of conveyance in connection with the importation or entry of livestock if the Secretary determines that the prohibition or restriction is necessary because the means of conveyance has not been maintained in a clean and sanitary condition or does not have accommodations for the safe and proper movement of livestock.

children.heading: Restrictions on import and entry
children.label: 1 Restrictions on import and entry
children.text: The Secretary may issue such orders and promulgate such regulations as are necessary to carry out subsection (a).

children.heading: Post importation quarantine
children.label: 2 Post importation quarantine
children.text: The Secretary may promulgate regulations requiring that any animal imported or entered be raised or handled under post-importation quarantine conditions by or under the supervision of the Secretary for the purpose of determining whether the animal is or may be affected by any pest or disease of livestock.

children.label: A
children.text: any animal, article, or means of conveyance that has been imported but has not entered the United States if the Secretary determines that destruction or removal from the United States is necessary to prevent the introduction into or dissemination within the United States of any pest or disease of livestock;

children.label: B
children.text: any animal or progeny of any animal, article, or means of conveyance that has been imported or entered in violation of this chapter; or

children.label: C
children.text: any animal that has strayed into the United States if the Secretary determines that destruction or removal from the United States is necessary to prevent the introduction into or dissemination within the United States of any pest or disease of livestock.

children.label: i
children.text: a means of conveyance used in connection with the importation of an animal;

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual involved in the importation of an animal and personal articles of the individual; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any article used in the importation of an animal.

children.heading: Failure to comply with orders
children.label: B Failure to comply with orders
i
children.text: take remedial action, destroy, or remove from the United States the animal or progeny of any animal, article, or means of conveyance as authorized under paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: recover from the owner the costs of any care, handling, disposal, or other action incurred by the Secretary in connection with the remedial action, destruction, or removal.
